title: Developing a European dimension to the sociology of education
abstract: Click to increase image sizeClick to decrease image size Notes 1. Even the new and more formalised method of the ‘open method of cooperation’ sets broad objectives rather than specific policy procedures, and allows states to devise their own means to achieve these standards (and a mid‐term review of Lisbon educational goals suggest that these objectives are unlikely to be met by the target date of 2010). 2. In the United States, this lens has been used by scholars in Stanford University to examine, for example, the convergence in curriculum subjects in education systems across the world (see Meyer & Ramirez, 2000 Meyer, J. W. and Ramirez, F. O. 2000. “The world institutionalization of education”. In Discourse formation in comparative education, Edited by: Schriewer, J. Frankfurt: Peter Lang. [Google Scholar]).
journal: British Journal of Sociology of Education
